name: Guerrero (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Guerrero centred.
history: Guerrero has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms are based on the pre-Columbian glyph for Tlachco/Tlachtli — the ball game — placed on a tepetl (hill) glyph, recalling the Aztec name from which the region's tribute roll derives, and honouring Vicente Guerrero, hero of independence and namesake of the state.
